message:

@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "lima-615-file-system-change-events-inotify-support-for-mounted-directories",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "lima: File system change events (inotify) support for mounted directories",
+    "description": "File system change events (inotify) support for mounted directories. Requested by 36+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current lima setup",
+        "description": "Verify your lima version and configuration:\n```bash\nlimactl --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working lima installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review lima configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant lima configuration:\n### Description\n\nFrom https://github.com/abiosoft/colima/issues/151:\n\nWe're using Colima to run Docker in a VM on our macOS laptops. Colima uses Lima under the hood to run this VM."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for File system change events (inotify) support for mounted…",
+        "description": "> As a personal workaround, that would work(just less performant, performance degrades linearly with the number of files being added).\n\nYeah, it's not ideal at all.  This is the easiest I could come up with, in case others need it:\n```\nFOLDER=src\nCHKPT=/tmp/checkpoint\ntouch $CHKPT\nwhile true; do\n \n```yaml\nFOLDER=src\nCHKPT=/tmp/checkpoint\ntouch $CHKPT\nwhile true; do\n  find \"$FOLDER\" -newer $CHKPT -type f -print -a -exec touch {} \\; | grep -q . && touch $CHKPT\n  sleep 1\ndone\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"File system change events (inotify) support for mounted…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> As a personal workaround, that would work(just less performant, performance degrades linearly with the number of files being added).\n\nYeah, it's not ideal at all.  This is the easiest I could come up with, in case others need it:\n```\nFOLDER=src\nCHKPT=/tmp/checkpoint\ntouch $CHKPT\nwhile true; do\n  find \"$FOLDER\" -newer $CHKPT -type f -print -a -exec touch {} \\; | grep -q .",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "FOLDER=src\nCHKPT=/tmp/checkpoint\ntouch $CHKPT\nwhile true; do\n  find \"$FOLDER\" -newer $CHKPT -type f -print -a -exec touch {} \\; | grep -q . && touch $CHKPT\n  sleep 1\ndone",
+        "FOLDER=$1\nTOUCH=\"docker exec -ti my-docker-guest touch\"\nCHKPT=/tmp/checkpoint\ntouch $CHKPT\nwhile true; do\n  find \"$FOLDER\" -newer $CHKPT -type f -print -a -exec $TOUCH {} \\; | grep . && touch $CHKPT\n  sleep 1\ndone"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
